hero_section.title:
It’s Time for Development that Lifts Everyone,
 Not Leaves Some Behind
hero_section.subtitle: Inequality is the result of policy choices.
hero_section.description: Since 1985, we have connected civil society voices with public policy. Advocating for fair, inclusive development and public policies that promote social justice, especially for vulnerable groups.
hero_section.aboutUsDescription1: The International NGO Forum on Indonesian Development (INFID) is a network of civil society organizations established in 1985 and committed to promoting equitable development, democracy, equality, and respect for human rights in Indonesia.
hero_section.aboutUsDescription2: INFID works through an evidence-based, inclusive advocacy approach, focusing on strategic issues such as reducing inequality, implementing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), and strengthening civil society and democracy.
hero_section.aboutUsDescription3: INFID has 80 members throughout Indonesia and is accredited by the United Nations (UN) and holds Special Consultative Status with ECOSOC at the UN.
hero_section.aboutUsOrganizationDescription: Working across the archipelago to collaboratively deepen understanding of public policies that advance social justice.
hero_section.impactTitle: Stories of change from working together in the field
hero_section.impactDescription: Each program brings real stories of change. Explore a summary of the impacts, then dive deeper into the full stories, just like reading a blog.

our_network.title: Power of Members
our_network.description: INFID welcomes organizations and individuals who are committed to and actively engaged in advancing Indonesia’s development.

section.title: OUR PROGRAMME
section.description: A comprehensive approach to addressing contemporary challenges.

human_rights.title: Civil Society for Human Rights & Democracy
human_rights.description: Strengthening the role of civil society in upholding human rights and ensuring a substantive and violence-free democratic process.

climate_governance.title: Democratic and Gender-Just Climate Governance
climate_governance.description: Promoting participatory, transparent and gender-responsive climate policies to ensure justice for vulnerable groups.

inclusive_development.title: Inclusive and Equitable Development
inclusive_development.description: Overseeing the development agenda to ensure equitable benefits, reduce inequality, and leave no one behind.

award_2017.title: 2017 Community Organisation Awards
award_2017.organization: Ministry of Home Affairs of the Republic of Indonesia
award_2017.description: Recipient of the Social Humanitarian Award for tangible contributions to community development.

ecosoc.title: Special Consultative Status
ecosoc.organization: UNITED NATIONS ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL COUNCIL
ecosoc.description: Official consultative status since 2004 (Ref. No: D1035), bringing Indonesia's voice to the global forum.

ran_pe_awards_2024.title: RAN PE Awards 2024
ran_pe_awards_2024.description: Award in the category "Multi-Stakeholder Collaboration Initiator"
